latest.title: IQE plc: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
1 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6490C
IQE PLC
01 May 2026
IQE plc
Total Voting Rights
Cardiff, UK
1 May 2026
("IQE" or the "Company")
Total Voting Rights
As at 30 April 2026, the Company's issued share capital consisted of 979,886,435 ordinary shares of 1 pence each ("Ordinary Shares"). The Company holds 4,115 Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number Ordinary Shares with voting rights in the Company is 979,882,320.
Shareholders may use this figure (979,882,320) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Contacts:
IQE plc
+44 (0) 29 2083 9400
Jutta Meier
Mark Cubitt
Amy Barlow
Peel Hunt (Nomad and Joint Broker)
+44 (0) 20 7418 8900
Ben Cryer
Kate Bannatyne
Adam Telling
Deutsche Numis (Joint Broker)
+44 (0) 20 7260 1000
Hugo Rubinstein
Iqra Amin
Headland Consultancy (Financial PR)
+ 44 (0) 20 38054822
Andy Rivett-Carnac: +44 (0) 7968 997 365
Chloe Francklin: +44 (0)78 3497 4624
ABOUT IQE
http://iqep.com
IQE is the leading global supplier of advanced compound semiconductor wafers and materials solutions that enable a diverse range of applications across:
· Smart Connected Devices
· Communications Infrastructure
· Automotive and Industrial
· Aerospace and Security
As a scaled global epitaxy wafer manufacturer, IQE is uniquely positioned in this market which has high barriers to entry. IQE supplies the global market and is enabling customers to innovate at chip and OEM level. By leveraging the Group's intellectual property portfolio including know-how and patents, it produces epitaxy wafers of superior quality, yield and unit economics.
IQE is headquartered in Cardiff UK, with employees across manufacturing locations in the UK, US and Taiwan, and is listed on the AIM Stock Exchange in London.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: IQE plc: Circular & Notice of General Meeting
latest.content:
Today 13:05
RNS Number : 4402C
IQE PLC
29 April 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT AND THE INFORMATION CONTAINED HEREIN IS RESTRICTED AND IS NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY IN, INTO OR FROM THE UNITED STATES OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD BREACH ANY APPLICABLE LAW OR REGULATION.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS FOR INFORMATION PURPOSES ONLY AND DOES NOT ITSELF CONSTITUTE A PROSPECTUS, AN OFFERING MEMORANDUM OR AN OFFER FOR SALE OR SUBSCRIPTION OF ANY SECURITIES IN THE COMPANY. THIS ANNOUNCEMENT AND DOES NOT CONSTITUTE OR CONTAIN ANY INVITATION, SOLICITATION, RECOMMENDATION, OFFER OR ADVICE TO ANY PERSON TO SUBSCRIBE FOR, OTHERWISE ACQUIRE OR DISPOSE OF ANY SECURITIES OF THE COMPANY IN ANY JURISDICTION IN WHICH ANY SUCH OFFER OR SOLICITATION WOULD BE UNLAWFUL.
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION AS DEFINED IN ARTICLE 7 OF REGULATION (EU) NO 596/2014 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL OF 16 APRIL 2014 ON MARKET ABUSE (MARKET ABUSE REGULATION) AS RETAINED AS PART OF UK LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 AS AMENDED.
IQE plc
Cardiff, UK
29 April 2026
Posting of Circular and Notice of General Meeting
IQE plc (AIM: IQE, "IQE", the "Company" and, together with its subsidiary companies, the "Group"),
a leading global supplier of compound semiconductor wafer products and advanced material solutions
,
is pleased to confirm that, further to the Company's announcement on 27 April 2026 in connection with the Proposed Fundraising, the Company has today published and posted to shareholders a circular ("
Circular
") containing a notice convening a general meeting of IQE (the "
General Meeting
").
The General Meeting is to be held
at the offices of White & Case LLP at 5 Old Broad Street, London EC2N 1DW at 9:30 a.m. on 15 May 2026
, at which
the
Fundraising Resolutions will be proposed: (i) by way of ordinary resolution, to approve the directors' authority to allot Ordinary Shares with an aggregate nominal value of up to £4,079,412.53, being equal to 407,941,253
new Ordinary Shares, in connection with and pursuant to the Proposed Fundraising; and (ii) by way of special resolution, empower the directors to allot and issue equity securities for cash on a non-pre-emptive basis with an aggregate nominal value of up to £4,079,412.53, being equal to 407,941,253
new Ordinary Shares, in connection with and pursuant to the Proposed Fundraising
.
The Proposed Fundraising is conditional,
inter alia
, upon Shareholders approving the Fundraising Resolutions at the General Meeting.
A copy of the Circular (including the Notice of General Meeting) and the Proxy Form are available on the Company's website at
https://www.iqe.com/investors/events/capital-raise-2026/
.
The Independent Directors consider the Fundraising Resolutions to be proposed at the General Meeting to be in the best interests of the Company and the Shareholders as a whole. Consequently, the Independent Directors unanimously recommend that you vote in favour of the Fundraising Resolutions to be proposed at the General Meeting, as they intend to do in respect of the 371,972 Existing Ordinary Shares held, directly or indirectly, by them representing approximately 0.04 per cent. of the total voting rights of the Company.
Shareholders should be aware that if the Fundraising Resolutions are not approved at the General Meeting, the Proposed Fundraising cannot complete and IQE will not receive the proceeds from the Proposed Fundraising. In such circumstances, the Company would be required to obtain emergency funding to meet its short-term liquidity needs and its financial position and prospects would be adversely affected.
Capitalised terms used but not defined in this announcement will have the same meaning given to them in the Circular.

ABOUT IQE
http://iqep.com
IQE is one of the leading global suppliers of advanced compound semiconductor wafers and materials solutions that enable a diverse range of applications across:
·
Smart Connected Devices
·
Communications Infrastructure
·
Automotive and Industrial
·
Aerospace and Security
As a scaled global epitaxy wafer manufacturer, IQE is uniquely positioned. IQE supplies the global market and is enabling customers to innovate at chip and OEM level. Through the Group's intellectual property portfolio including know-how and patents, it produces epitaxy wafers of superior quality, yield and unit economics.
IQE is headquartered in Cardiff UK, with employees across manufacturing locations in the UK, US and Taiwan, and is quoted on the AIM Market of the London Stock Exchange.

company_30s.what_they_do: IQE plc designs and builds silicon photonics wafers – the tiny glass‑like chips that let lasers and data streams move faster inside phones, data‑centres and high‑speed fibre networks.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The world’s data traffic is exploding. Photonics is the backbone of 5G, AI‑driven servers and future quantum links. IQE’s wafers are a critical, hard‑to‑replace component that keeps those systems running.
company_30s.current_state: The business is still turning a loss and relies on external financing and partnerships. It has a modest pipeline of orders but is still in a high‑cost, low‑margin phase. The stock sits well below its historic valuation, signalling a steep discount to the company’s fundamentals.

rns_narrative: The latest filings focus on compliance and governance – a routine Form 8.3 and a Rule 2.9 announcement that confirm the company is meeting regulatory deadlines.
These documents hint at a pause in aggressive spending and a shift toward stabilising cash flow, but they also flag a need to raise fresh capital to keep production lines running.
In short, IQE is tightening its belt while keeping the door open for new investors or strategic partners.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet hope – the community is watching but not yet talking.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to the photonics boom and the company’s niche position, saying a price rebound is inevitable once funding is secured.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the high burn rate and lack of revenue could lead to a liquidity crunch, especially if the market for photonics slows.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from cautious curiosity to a low‑volume, mixed‑tone discussion. The buzz is still muted, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search activity for IQE, meaning the market is largely unaware of any forthcoming catalysts.

near_term_catalysts: A funding announcement or a new strategic partnership could inject the cash needed to ramp up production.
An earnings release that shows a modest revenue uptick or a reduction in losses would signal that the company is stabilising.
A volume spike on a key support level (around the 10‑day moving average) would confirm that traders are stepping in after capitulation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 3‑to‑4‑fold increase in daily volume, a bounce off the 10‑day SMA, and any mention of new customer contracts or cost‑cutting initiatives in the next RNS.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is deep in a crash phase – most of the sell‑off is already done. If a catalyst appears, the price is likely to start a mean‑reversion run from its bottom. The APEX score of 8.0 signals a tight compression, so a breakout could be quick.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: IQE is 70‑plus percent below its pre‑crash level. A rebound to 50% of that peak would deliver a 100% upside. The photonics market is growing, and a successful funding round could unlock production capacity and revenue streams.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still has a negative cash flow and no proven revenue. If it fails to secure additional capital, the price could collapse further or the firm could be forced to liquidate assets.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 0.09 p → 0.00 p (100% down). Potential gain: 0.09 p → 0.15 p (66% upside). The upside is roughly 2.5 times the downside, making the trade highly asymmetric.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price touches the 10‑day SMA (~0.08 p) and volume is at least 3× the 20‑day average. Size a position that uses no more than 10 % of your discretionary capital. Set a stop 0.02 p below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a clear volume spike and a bullish candle close on or above the 10‑day SMA. If the price stays below 0.07 p for more than a week, consider tightening the stop or exiting.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals, so the play remains bullish as long as the company continues to raise capital or secure contracts.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IQE sits deep in a crash, the market is silent, and a funding or contract win could trigger a sharp mean‑reversion – a high‑reward, asymmetric contrarian opportunity.
